Level 1: Journal describes citation of data in guidelines to authors with clear rules and examples.
Level 2: Article requires appropriate citation for data and materials used consistent with the journal's author guidelines.
Level 3: Article is not published until providing appropriate citation for data and materials following journal's author guidelines

Level 0: Data sharing is encouraged, or not mentioned
Level 1: Articles must state whether or not data are available.
Level 2: Articles must have publicly available data, or an explanation why ethical or legal constraints prevent it.
Level 3: Articles must have publicly available data and must be used to computationally reproduce or confirm results prior to publication

Level 0: Code sharing is encouraged, or not mentioned
Level 1: Articles must state whether or not code is available. Requiring a code availability statement satisfies this level
Level 2: Articles must have publicly available code, or an explanation why ethical or legal constraints prevent it.
Level 3: Articles must have publicly available code and must be used to computationally reproduce or confirm results prior to publication

Level 0: Materials sharing is encouraged, or not mentioned
Level 1: Articles must state whether or not materials are available. Requiring a materials availability statement satisfies this level
Level 2: Articles must have publicly available materials, or an explanation why ethical or legal constraints prevent it.
Level 3: Articles must have publicly available materials and must be used to computationally reproduce or confirm results prior to publication

Area: Reporting guidelines AKA Design and Analysis Transparency
Level 2: Journal requires adherence to design transparency standards for review and publication.
Level 3: Journal requires and enforces adherence to design transparency standards for review and publication.

Level 1: Articles will state if work was preregistered.
Level 2: Article states whether work was preregistered and, if so, journal verifies adherence to preregistered plan.
Level 3: Journal requires that confirmatory or inferential research must be preregistered.

Level 1: Articles will state if work was preregistered with an analysis plan.
Level 2: Article states whether work was preregistered with an analysis plan and, if so, journal verifies adherence to preregistered plan.
Level 3: Journal requires that confirmatory or inferential research must be preregistered with an analysis plan.

Level 1: Journal encourages submission of replication studies.
Level 2: Journal will review replication studies blinded to results.
Level 3: Registered Reports for replications as a regular submission option.

Level 1: Journal states that significance or novelty are not a criteria for publication decisions.
Level 2: Journal will review studies blinded to results.
Level 3: Journal accepts Registered Reports for novel studies as a regular submission option.

Level 1: Journal awards 1 or 2 open science badges
Level 2: Journal awards all 3 open science badges
